digest.component_or_area: MCP auth trusted-proxy verifier + header-guard middleware
propositions: Add a trusted-proxy auth mode activated by including 'proxy' in mcp.auth_strategies, delegating authentication to a fronting edge proxy.

An ASGI middleware validates a shared-secret header with constant-time compare and only then extracts the identity header into a request-scoped principal, stripping the entire X-Forwarded-* family (with case/underscore normalization) otherwise.

A TokenVerifier-shaped component mints an AccessToken with client_id=identity, scopes=auth_proxy_scopes, and resource=auth_resource_server_url so proxy identity flows through the same composite audience check.
auth_proxy_scopes defaults to an empty set, so a proxy principal holds no scopes and is denied 403 by any non-empty auth_required_scopes until configured.
The verifier fails closed: it refuses to start when the env var named by auth_proxy_secret_env is absent or empty, and returns None (401/deny) when no valid secret and identity are present.
New auth_proxy_* config fields and derived REBAR_MCP_AUTH_PROXY_* env-var names are registered in schema, mcp_server, and server.json so the manifest drift check passes.
